source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning Service Account Related Roles to Users

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Separation of Duties Is Enforced While Assigning Service Account Related Roles to Users
input_row.description: It is recommended that the principle of 'Separation of Duties' is enforced while assigning service-account related roles to users.
input_row.rationale: The built-in/predefined IAM role Service Account admin allows the user/identity to create, delete, and manage service account(s). The built-in/predefined IAM role Service Account User allows the user/identity (with adequate privileges on Compute and App Engine) to assign service account(s) to Apps/Compute Instances. Separation of duties is the concept of ensuring that one individual does not have all necessary permissions to be able to complete a malicious action. In Cloud IAM - service accounts, this could be an action such as using a service account to access resources that user should not normally have access to. Separation of duties is a business control typically used in larger organizations, meant to help avoid security or privacy incidents and errors. It is considered best practice. No user should have Service Account Admin and Service Account User roles assigned at the same time. Impact: The removed role should be assigned to a different user based on business needs.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam. 2. Ensure no member has the roles Service Account Admin and Service account User assigned together. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all users and role assignments: gcloud projects get-iam-policy [Project_ID] --format json | \ jq -r '[ (["Service_Account_Admin_and_User"] | (., map(length*"-"))), ( [ .bindings[] | select(.role == "roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin" or .role == "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser").members[] ] | group_by(.) | map({User: ., Count: length}) | .[] | select(.Count == 2).User | unique ) ] | .[] | @tsv' 2. All common users listed under Service_Account_Admin_and_User are assigned both the roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin and roles/iam.serviceAccountUser roles.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to IAM & Admin/IAM using https://console.cloud.google.com/iam- admin/iam. 2. For any member having both Service Account Admin and Service account User roles granted/assigned, click the Delete Bin icon to remove either role from the member. Removal of a role should be done based on the business requirements. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts 2. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles 3. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/granting-roles-to-service-accounts Additional Information: Users granted with Owner (roles/owner) and Editor (roles/editor) have privileges equivalent to Service Account Admin and Service Account User. To avoid the misuse, Owner and Editor roles should be granted to very limited users and Use of these primitive privileges should be minimal. These requirements are addressed in separate recommendations.
input_row.impact: The removed role should be assigned to a different user based on business needs.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts 2. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles 3. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/granting-roles-to-service-accounts Additional Information: Users granted with Owner (roles/owner) and Editor (roles/editor) have privileges equivalent to Service Account Admin and Service Account User. To avoid the misuse, Owner and Editor roles should be granted to very limited users and Use of these primitive privileges should be minimal. These requirements are addressed in separate recommendations.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be fully verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands, making it suitable for an automated audit approach. The control revolves around checking the IAM roles assigned to users in a GCP project, specifically ensuring that no user has both the `Service Account Admin` and `Service Account User` roles simultaneously. This task is well-suited for automation as the IAM settings can be queried directly using GCP CLI tools.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_iam_roles_service_account_separation_of_duties_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP API Calls/Google Cloud SDK Methods**: Use `gcloud iam roles` and `gcloud projects get-iam-policy`
- **GCP CLI Commands**: 
  - Retrieve the list of all IAM roles assigned in a project using:
    ```bash
    gcloud projects get-iam-policy [Project_ID] --format=json
    ```
  - Use `jq` or an equivalent tool to process the JSON output and identify users holding both `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser`.
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate**: All IAM role bindings in the specified GCP project.
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate**: Check users with both roles `roles/iam.serviceAccountAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser` assigned concurrently.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
  - **Pass**: No users have both `Service Account Admin` and `Service Account User` roles assigned.
  - **Fail**: One or more users have both roles assigned.

This automated approach ensures that separation of duties is enforced for service account-related roles, enhancing security and minimizing the risk of unauthorized access or misuse.
